sharedStateObservations.observation: The checkpoint ID generation uses timestamp-based format (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS-MS) which ensures chronological sorting. The checkpoint adapter correctly returns newest-first by sorting and reversing the filename list.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/adapters/checkpoint-store.adapter.ts:generateCheckpointId() creates ${y}${m}${d}-${h}${min}${s}-${ms} format; list() method does .sort().reverse() for newest-first ordering

sharedStateObservations.observation: All source files follow TypeScript Style Guide from AGENTS.md: interfaces for object shapes, PascalCase for types/interfaces, camelCase for variables/functions, readonly for immutable data, and explicit return types for public API functions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SaveCheckpointResult, ListCheckpointsResult, LoadCheckpointResult are interfaces (not types). Checkpoint interface uses Readonly<Record<...>>. All exported usecase functions have explicit return types.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Import grouping follows AGENTS.md: built-in first (node:fs/promises), then external (commander), then internal ports/usecases, then relative domain files. No import * patterns used.

summary: The checkpoint-cli-lifecycle feature has been implemented correctly and thoroughly. The worker followed all documented patterns: backend-worker skill procedure was executed properly with 29 tests passing (13 unit + 16 integration). The implementation covers all requirements from the feature description: timestamped snapshots with current milestone and feature/assertion states, newest-first listing, latest checkpoint on load, and prominent metadata-only restore warning. Code follows AGENTS.md TypeScript conventions, respects architecture.md storage boundaries, and aligns with implementation-patterns.md for command structure and --json inheritance. No issues or deviations found.
